---
title: "About CitySchools Collaborative (CSC)"
subtitle: "Building the systems that help schools make tutoring work"
format: html
editor: visual


---

## Who We Are

CitySchools Collaborative (CSC) is a nonprofit organization dedicated to expanding access to high-quality, high-impact tutoring for students who need it most. Based in Washington, DC, CSC works directly with schools and districts to build the planning infrastructure, coaching support, and quality monitoring systems that allow tutoring programs to succeed. CSC is DC's trusted partner in citywide initiatives related to tutoring, talent, and truancy, having served over 100 schools since 2021.

---

## Our Approach

CSC's model goes beyond placing tutors in classrooms. We partner with schools to:

- Align tutoring to the Science of Reading and to classroom instruction
- Build consistent, protected time for tutoring into the school day
- Monitor implementation to catch problems early
- Use data to track progress and inform decision-making

This comprehensive approach is what the IST study is designed to evaluate, and what CSC believes makes high-impact tutoring truly high-impact.

Read more about us at [cityschoolscollab.org](https://cityschoolscollab.org/).

---

## Who CSC Has Served

CSC has worked with over 100 schools since 2020, including:

:::::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="33%"}
1. Achievement Prep PCS
2. Anacostia High School
3. Ballou High School
4. Ballou STAY High School
5. Bancroft Elementary School
6. Bard High School Early College DC
7. Bridges PCS
8. Brookland Middle School
9. Capital City PCS – High School
10. Capital City PCS – Lower School
11. Capital City PCS – Middle School
12. Capital Village PCS
13. Cardozo Education Campus
14. Center City PCS – Brightwood
15. Center City PCS – Shaw
16. Cesar Chavez Public Charter Schools for Public Policy
17. Columbia Heights Education Campus
18. DC Prep PCS
19. DC Scholars PCS
20. Deal Middle School
21. Digital Pioneers Academy PCS – Capitol Hill
22. District of Columbia International School
23. Drew Elementary School
24. Dunbar High School
25. E.L. Haynes PCS – Elementary School
26. E.L. Haynes PCS – High School
27. E.L. Haynes PCS – Middle School
28. Eagle Academy PCS – Congress Heights
29. Eastern High School
30. Eliot-Hine Middle School
31. Friendship PCS – Armstrong Elementary
32. Friendship PCS – Armstrong Middle
33. Friendship PCS – Blow Pierce Elementary
34. Friendship PCS – Blow Pierce Middle
35. Friendship PCS – Technology Preparatory High School
36. Garrison Elementary School
37. Girls Global Academy PCS
38. Global Citizens Public Charter School
:::
::: {.column width="33%"}
39. H.D. Woodson High School
40. Hart Middle School
41. Hearst Elementary School
42. Hendley Elementary School
43. Hope Community PCS
44. Houston Elementary School
45. Howard University Middle School of Mathematics and Science PCS
46. I Dream PCS
47. IDEA Public Charter School
48. Ida B. Wells Middle School
49. Ingenuity Prep PCS
50. Inspired Teaching Demonstration PCS
51. Johnson Middle School
52. Kelly Miller Middle School
53. Ketcham Elementary School
54. King Elementary School
55. Kingsman Academy PCS
56. KIPP DC – Promise Academy PCS
57. KIPP DC – WILL Academy PCS
58. Kramer Middle School
59. Langley Elementary School
60. Latin American Montessori Bilingual PCS
61. Lawrence E. Boone Elementary School
62. Lee Montessori PCS – Brookland
63. Luke C. Moore High School
64. Macfarland Middle School
65. Maya Angelou Academy @ Youth Services Center
66. Maya Angelou PCS – High School
67. McKinley Technology High School
68. Meridian PCS
69. Miller Middle School
70. Monument Academy PCS
71. Moten Elementary School
72. Mundo Verde Bilingual PCS – Calle Ocho
73. Mundo Verde Bilingual PCS – J.F. Cook
74. Noyes Elementary School
75. Patterson Elementary School
76. Paul PCS – International High School
77. Payne Elementary School
78. Peabody Elementary School
:::
::: {.column width="33%"}
79. Perry Street Preparatory PCS
80. Plummer Elementary School
81. Powell Elementary School
82. Randle Highlands Elementary School
83. Raymond Elementary School
84. (R)evolution Montessori
85. Ron Brown College Preparatory High School
86. Roosevelt STAY High School
87. Simon Elementary School
88. Smothers Elementary School
89. Social Justice PCS
90. Sojourner Truth PCS
91. Sousa Middle School
92. Stanton Elementary School
93. Statesmen College Preparatory Academy for Boys PCS
94. Stuart-Hobson Middle School
95. Thomas Elementary School
96. Thurgood Marshall Academy PCS
97. Truesdell Education Campus
98. Tubman Elementary School
99. Turner Elementary School
100. Two Rivers PCS – 4th Street
101. Tyler Elementary School
102. Van Ness Elementary School
103. Walker-Jones Education Campus
104. Washington Global PCS
105. Washington Latin PCS
106. Washington Leadership Academy PCS
107. Washington Yu Ying PCS
108. Wheatley Education Campus
109. Whitlock Elementary School
110. Wildflower Public Charter Schools
111. Wilson High School
:::
::::::

Read more about how CSC has impacted these schools in their [annual impact reports](https://cityschoolscollab.org/).

---

## Proven Results

A previous study of CSC's tutoring program found that students who received high-intensity math tutoring from CSC providers in 2023–2025 received the equivalent of an additional 59 instructional days and achieved, on average, 138% of their expected growth on math assessments.

[→ Read the QED Report](https://www.empowerk12.org/research-source/2025-csc-hit-impact-evaluation)
---

## Quarto

Quarto enables you to weave together content and executable code into a finished document. To learn more about Quarto see <https://quarto.org>.

## Running Code

When you click the **Render** button a document will be generated that includes both content and the output of embedded code. You can embed code like this:

```{r}
1 + 1
```

You can add options to executable code like this

```{r}
#| echo: false
2 * 2
```

The `echo: false` option disables the printing of code (only output is displayed).
